sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The centers don't change between initial and current state. So the centers are fixed properties of each grid.
Let me think about the center patterns more carefully:
TR center (most complete):
g W g
W R W
g W g
This is a nice symmetric pattern with 9 cells, all 4 pixels each.
TL center:
g W g(8)
W R ?
g W(8)
Some cells are merged (8 pixels instead of 4), suggesting missing cells.
BL center:
W(8) g(8)
? R ?
g(8) ?
More merged cells.
BR center:
W(12) g(8)
? R W
? g(8)
Also has merged cells.
The fact that TR has the most complete center (all 9 cells present as separate 4-pixel objects) suggests TR is the "template" or "reference" grid.
But I already tried matching TR's outer pattern and it didn't work.
Let me think about this differently. What if the goal is to make BR's OUTER pattern match the grid that has the SAME center pattern as BR?
But BR's center is different from all three source centers...
Wait, let me look at the center patterns more carefully. Maybe there's a relationship:
TR center: gWg / WRW / gWg (complete, symmetric)
TL center: gWg / WR? / gW? (missing bottom-right corner)
BL center: Wg? / ?R? / g?? (missing many cells)
BR center: Wg? / ?RW / ?g? (missing some cells)
Hmm, what if the center represents which outer cells are "active" or "present"? Like, the center is a mini-map of the outer pattern?
TR center has all 9 cells → all 8 outer cells are present (plus center)
TL center has 7 cells → 7 outer cells present
BL center has 5 cells → 5 outer cells present
BR center has 6 cells → 6 outer cells present
But all grids have all 8 outer cells visible...
Let me try a completely different approach. What if I need to click on the SOURCE GRIDS to select which one to copy? Like, click on TR first, then the pattern gets copied to BR?
Let me try clicking on the center of TR grid.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Centers are fixed and different. TR has the most complete center (all 9 cells). Maybe I need to click on a source grid to "select" it as the template. Let me try clicking on TR's center.
